metadata.title: Top 100+ Process Mining Researchers Worldwide
sources: IEEE Task Force on Process Mining (tf-pm.org)
metadata.notes: Affiliations reflect best-known positions as of 2024-2025. Some researchers hold dual affiliations (academia + industry).

researchers.name: Wil van der Aalst

notable_publications.title: Process Mining: Data Science in Action
Workflow Mining: Discovering Process Models from Event Logs
Process Mining Manifesto

researchers.bio: Known as the 'Godfather of Process Mining', van der Aalst is the founder of the discipline. H-index 188, over 170,000 citations. ACM/IEEE/IFIP Fellow and member of multiple national academies of science.

researchers.name: Boudewijn van Dongen

notable_publications.title: The ProM Framework: A New Era in Process Mining Tool Support
Conformance Checking of Processes Based on Monitoring Real Behavior
A General Divide and Conquer Approach for Process Mining

researchers.bio: Full Professor and Chair of the Process Analytics group at TU/e. Lead developer of the ProM process mining framework and XES event log standard.

researchers.name: Josep Carmona

notable_publications.title: Conformance Checking: Relating Processes and Models
Conformance Checking: Foundations, Milestones and Challenges
A Fresh Look at Precision in Process Conformance

researchers.bio: Associate Professor at UPC Barcelona, leading expert on conformance checking and alignment-based techniques in process mining.

researchers.name: Marlon Dumas

notable_publications.title: Fundamentals of Business Process Management
Outcome-Oriented Predictive Process Monitoring: Review and Benchmark
Split Miner: Automated Discovery of Accurate and Simple Business Process Models from Event Logs

researchers.bio: Full Professor of Software Engineering at University of Tartu, co-author of the widely-used BPM textbook. Leads the BPM Research Group in Tartu.

researchers.name: Marcello La Rosa
researchers.department: School of Computing and Information Systems

notable_publications.title: Split Miner: Automated Discovery of Accurate and Simple Business Process Models from Event Logs
Fundamentals of Business Process Management
BPMN Miner: Automated Discovery of BPMN Process Models with Hierarchical Structure

researchers.bio: Professor of Information Systems at University of Melbourne. Founder and CEO of Apromore (acquired by Salesforce), a leading process mining platform.

researchers.name: Dirk Fahland
researchers.department: Department of Mathematics and Computer Science

notable_publications.title: Multi-Dimensional Process Discovery
Simplifying Discovered Process Models in a Controlled Manner
Object-Centric Process Mining: Dealing with Divergence and Convergence in Event Data

researchers.bio: Associate Professor at TU/e, key contributor to object-centric process mining and advanced process discovery techniques.

researchers.name: Jan Mendling

notable_publications.title: Fundamentals of Business Process Management
Blockchains for Business Process Management - Challenges and Opportunities
Metrics for Process Models: A Systematic Literature Review

researchers.bio: Full Professor of Information Systems at HU Berlin. Co-author of the leading BPM textbook, prolific researcher in process model quality and analytics.

researchers.name: Hajo A. Reijers
researchers.department: Department of Information and Computing Sciences

notable_publications.title: Fundamentals of Business Process Management
Best Practices in Business Process Redesign: An Overview and Qualitative Evaluation
The Biggest Business Process Management Problems to Solve Before We Die

researchers.bio: Full Professor at Utrecht University, leading the BPM & Analytics group. Co-author of the standard BPM textbook.

researchers.name: Mathias Weske
researchers.affiliation: Hasso Plattner Institute, University of Potsdam

notable_publications.title: Business Process Management: Concepts, Languages, Architectures
Blockchains for Business Process Management - Challenges and Opportunities
Process Mining with ProM

researchers.bio: Full Professor at HPI Potsdam, leading the Business Process Technology group. Author of a widely-used BPM textbook.

researchers.name: Manfred Reichert
researchers.department: Institute of Databases and Information Systems

notable_publications.title: The Biggest Business Process Management Problems to Solve Before We Die
